title: A transparent calibration protocol for airborne methane sensors
abstract: We present a calibration protocol for airborne methane sensors and show that it reduces drift by 14% relative to the current baseline across three field deployments. The manuscript names the failure regime during sustained humidity spikes, reports uncertainty intervals, and releases code and data.
manuscript_text: We benchmark the protocol across three airborne methane surveys using the same reference gas workflow. Table 1 reports pre-calibration and post-calibration error with confidence intervals. Figure 1 shows drift reduction for each deployment, and Figure 2 shows the humidity-driven failure regime above 88% relative humidity. The method should not be used outside the validated humidity window. We compare against the current baseline and document the open data, code, and materials pathway.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Drift reduction across the three airborne deployments after calibration.
Figure 2. Failure regime observed during sustained humidity spikes above 88% relative humidity.
tables: Table 1. Pre-calibration and post-calibration error with confidence intervals.
references: Ref1 baseline airborne methane calibration workflow.
Ref2 field validation protocol for mobile gas sensing.
Ref3 uncertainty reporting for atmospheric sensor calibration.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A lists deployment settings, calibration gas composition, and instrument intervals.
data_availability: Field deployment data are archived in a public repository.
code_availability: Calibration scripts are archived in a public repository.
materials_availability: Reference gas workflow and materials list are included in the appendix.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
